free_text: Measurements and analysis of angular distribution
interference effects in the 7Li(p,gamma)8Be reaction
Two different Van de Graaff accelerators.
The first, capable of producing a maximum energy of
2 MeV, was used to obtain the angular distribution data
from Ep = 800 keV to 1700 keV. The second, an H.V.E.C.
3 MeV machine, was used to obtain the 0 degr.
excitation curve from Ep=420 keV to 1850 keV.
Energy resolution for both cases.
Elemental lithium onto 0.025 cm gold backings
The detectors used were NaI(Tl) crystals.
One: 7.68cm diam x7.68cm - it served as monitor (was
fixed at 90 degr.) and another: 12.8cm diam. x12.8cm
on a movable arm.
Measurements were made at angles of 0, 30, 45, 60, 90,
120, 135 degr, chosen in random order.
Compilation produced by RFNC-VNIIEF Sarov
